---
title: "data_description"
author: "Wendy Shue"
date: "2026-03-26"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(knitr)
library(gtsummary)
library(dplyr)
library(here)
library(kableExtra)
```

```{r create-data-dict, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load the necessary library
library(knitr)

# Create the data frame with your variables
data_dict <- data.frame(
  variable_name = c(
    "agemon", "childid", "chsex", "dint", 
    "drwaterq_new", "round", "yc", "zhfa"
  ),
  variable_description = c(
    "Child’s age (in months)", 
    "Child ID", 
    "Child’s sex", 
    "Date of interview", 
    "Access to safe drinking water", 
    "Round of survey", 
    "Younger cohort = 1; Older cohort = 0", 
    "Height-for-age z-score"
  )
)

# Generate the table
kable(
  data_dict, 
  col.names = c("Variable Name", "Variable Description"), # Cleans up the headers
  caption = "Dataset Variable Dictionary"                 # Optional table caption
) |> 
  kable_styling()
```
```{r}
setwd(here())
ethiopia <- read.csv(paste0(here(),"/data/processed/ethiopia_processed.csv")) |> 
  dplyr::select(!childid) |> 
  mutate(chsex=factor(chsex,level=c(1,2), label=c("F","M")))
yc <- ethiopia |> dplyr::filter(yc==1) |> dplyr::select(round,agemon,chsex,zhfa, drwaterq_new)
oc <- ethiopia |> dplyr::filter(yc==0) |> dplyr::select(round,agemon,chsex,zhfa, drwaterq_new)
tbl_summary(yc, by=round,
            missing_text = "Missing", missing_stat = "{p_miss}") |> 
  modify_caption("**Younger cohort**")

tbl_summary(oc, by=round,
            missing_text = "Missing", missing_stat = "{p_miss}") |> 
  modify_caption("**Older cohort**")
```
